title: NiSource Stock Looks Fairly Valued
text: NiSource Inc. is an $11-billion market-cap energy holding company operating as a regulated natural gas and electric utility in the United States. The company reported strong financial results for Q1 2023, with an adjusted net income of $343.0 million or $0.77 per share, beating the consensus forecast. The company is actively transitioning its generation portfolio to retire all coal-fired generation by the end of 2028 and plans to invest heavily in capital expenditures.

title: 4 Solid Stocks to Bet on in a Resilient Labor Market
text: Given the market volatility, it would be wise to invest in defensive stocks like PPL Corporation (PPL), NiSource Inc. (NI), Ingredion Incorporated (INGR) and Lamb Weston Holdings, Inc. (LW).

title: 5 Stocks to Buy as Fed Continues to Grapple With Inflation
text: The Fed has indicated at least two more interest rate hikes this year as inflation is still high. Invest in utility stocks like NiSource (NI), Spire (SR), PPL Corporation (PPL), Atmos Energy Corporation (ATO) and Northwest Natural Holding Company (NWN).

title: 3 Reasons Growth Investors Will Love NiSource (NI)
text: NiSource (NI) is well positioned to outperform the market, as it exhibits above-average growth in financials.

title: NiSource (NI) to Sell $2.15B Stake in NIPSCO to Blackstone
text: NiSource's (NI) unit monetizes a portion of NIPSCO and utilizes the fund to cut down debts and invest in clean power projects.

title: NiSource stock gains after deal to sell about 20% stake in Nipsco for $2.2 billion to Blackstone
text: Shares of NiSource Inc. NI, +0.15% rose 0.4% toward a one-month high in premarket trading Tuesday, after the Indiana-based gas and electric utility announced an agreement to sell 19.9% of its Nipsco business to Blackstone Inc. BX, -1.64% for $2.15 billion. NiSource plans to use the proceeds from the sale of a minority stake in the natural gas and electric company to pay down debt and fund capital needs related to the renewable generation transition.

title: NiSource announces agreement to sell minority equity interest in NIPSCO to strengthen financial foundation and support sustainable, long-term growth
text: Blackstone Infrastructure Partners affiliate agrees to acquire 19.9% non-controlling equity interest in NIPSCO for $2.150 Billion, with additional equity commitment of $250 million to fund ongoing capital requirements. Blackstone invests alongside NiSource in NIPSCO to fund the energy transition and accelerate the reindustrialization of the Midwest.

title: Reasons to Add NiSource (NI) to Your Portfolio Right Now
text: NiSource (NI) makes a strong case for investment, given its positive growth estimates, stable ROE and strong liquidity.

title: NI vs. MGEE: Which Stock Is the Better Value Option?
text: Investors looking for stocks in the Utility - Electric Power sector might want to consider either NiSource (NI) or MGE (MGEE). But which of these two stocks offers value investors a better bang for their buck right now?

title: Too much debt coming due and not much cash on hand: These 10 companies face a rough year ahead
text: Jefferies & Co. offered a list of the top 10 companies with low cash and high near-term debt maturities this week that it's advising investors to avoid.
